title: TradeDecisionV0
description: Fine-tune target schema for Qwen3.5-7B polymarket/crypto trading-decision LoRA. Mirrors polymarket-agents `RefereeOutput` (committee/contracts.py) with optional crypto extensions. The fine-tuned model emits one JSON object matching this schema per inference. Strict-mode Pydantic loaders should validate with `extra=forbid`.

summary.description: 1-3 sentence high-signal summary of the decision and why.

global_confidence.description: Overall calibrated confidence the chosen side will resolve correctly. NOT the same as max(probability) — should reflect data quality and disagreement, not just argmax.

choice_assessments.description: Per-outcome probability table. For polymarket binary markets: 2 entries (YES, NO). For crypto: 2 entries (LONG, SHORT) or 3 entries (LONG, SHORT, HOLD). Probabilities MUST sum to 1.0 ± 0.01.

label.description: Outcome label. Polymarket: matches market outcome (e.g. 'YES', 'NO', 'Trump', 'Harris'). Crypto: 'LONG' | 'SHORT' | 'HOLD'.

confidence.description: Calibrated confidence in THIS specific probability estimate. Lower when evidence is thin or contradictory.

data_coverage.description: Fraction of relevant signal categories that had usable data (news, market state, on-chain, etc).

contradictions.description: Explicit notes about contradicting evidence we considered.

key_points.description: Bullet-style key drivers for this assessment. 1-5 items typical.

uncertainty_flags.description: Controlled-vocab uncertainty flags (mirrors polymarket-agents UNCERTAINTY_FLAGS_ENUM). Unknown values rejected.

trade_blockers.description: Controlled-vocab reasons the decision should NOT be executed even if probabilities favor a side.

rationale.description: Multi-paragraph reasoning trace. This is what the fine-tuned model is being taught to emit — explicit step-by-step justification linking evidence to probability. Used downstream for audit + human review.

crypto_extension.description: Optional crypto-specific fields. Null/absent for polymarket markets. Populated for crypto signals consumed by crypto-trader/bot/strategies/base.py Signal.

timefm_score.description: TimesFM directional score if used. Null if not consulted.

$comment: Invariants (enforced by Pydantic validator, not JSON schema): sum(choice_assessments[].probability) in [0.99, 1.01]; choice_assessments[].label unique within object. argmax(probability) is the implicit recommended_side — model MUST NOT emit a separate 'recommended_side' field, derive it.
